failures.question: From race no. 50 to 100, how many finishers have been disqualified?
failures.gold_sql: SELECT SUM(IIF(time IS NOT NULL, 1, 0)) FROM results WHERE statusId = 2 AND raceID < 100 AND raceId > 50
failures.final_sql:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS disqualified_finishers
FROM results
WHERE raceId BETWEEN 50 AND 100
  AND statusId = 2;

history.sql:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS disqualified_finishers
FROM results
WHERE raceId BETWEEN 50 AND 100
  AND statusId = 2;

failures.question: What is the complete address of the school with the lowest excellence rate? Indicate the Street, City, Zip and State.
failures.gold_sql: SELECT T2.Street, T2.City, T2.State, T2.Zip FROM satscores AS T1 INNER JOIN schools AS T2 ON T1.cds = T2.CDSCode ORDER BY CAST(T1.NumGE1500 AS REAL) / T1.NumTstTakr ASC LIMIT 1
failures.final_sql:
SELECT s.Street, s.City, s.Zip, s.State
FROM schools s
JOIN satscores ss ON s.CDSCode = ss.cds
ORDER BY CAST(ss.NumGE1500 AS FLOAT) / ss.NumTstTakr ASC
LIMIT 1;

history.sql:
SELECT s.Street, s.City, s.Zip, s.State
FROM schools s
JOIN satscores ss ON s.CDSCode = ss.cds
ORDER BY CAST(ss.NumGE1500 AS FLOAT) / ss.NumTstTakr ASC
LIMIT 1;

failures.question: Calculate the percentage of carcinogenic molecules which contain the Chlorine element.
failures.gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(CASE WHEN T2.label = '+' AND T1.element = 'cl' THEN T2.molecule_id ELSE NULL END) * 100 / COUNT(T2.molecule_id) FROM atom AS T1 INNER JOIN molecule AS T2 ON T1.molecule_id = T2.molecule_id
failures.final_sql:
SELECT 
  CAST(COUNT(CASE WHEN m.label = '+' THEN 1 END) AS FLOAT) * 100.0 / COUNT(*) AS percentage
FROM 
  molecule m
  JOIN atom a ON m.molecule_id = a.molecule_id
WHERE 
  a.element = 'cl'
  AND m.label = '+';

history.sql:
SELECT 
  CAST(COUNT(CASE WHEN m.label = '+' THEN 1 END) AS FLOAT) * 100.0 / COUNT(*) AS percentage
FROM 
  molecule m
  JOIN atom a ON m.molecule_id = a.molecule_id
WHERE 
  a.element = 'cl'
  AND m.label = '+';

failures.question: For all patients with normal uric acid (UA), what is the average UA index based on their latest laboratory examination result?
failures.gold_sql: SELECT AVG(T2.UA) FROM Patient AS T1 INNER JOIN Laboratory AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.ID WHERE (T2.UA < 6.5 AND T1.SEX = 'F') OR (T2.UA < 8.0 AND T1.SEX = 'M') AND T2.Date = ( SELECT MAX(Date) FROM Laboratory )
failures.final_sql:
SELECT AVG(l."UA") AS average_UA
FROM "Laboratory" l
JOIN "Patient" p ON l."ID" = p."ID"
WHERE (p."SEX" = 'female' AND l."UA" < 6.5)
   OR (p."SEX" = 'male' AND l."UA" < 8.0)
  AND l."Date" = (
    SELECT MAX(l2."Date")
    FROM "Laboratory" l2
    WHERE l2."ID" = l."ID"
  );

history.sql:
SELECT AVG(l."UA") AS average_UA
FROM "Laboratory" l
JOIN "Patient" p ON l."ID" = p."ID"
WHERE (p."SEX" = 'female' AND l."UA" < 6.5)
   OR (p."SEX" = 'male' AND l."UA" < 8.0)
  AND l."Date" = (
    SELECT MAX(l2."Date")
    FROM "Laboratory" l2
    WHERE l2."ID" = l."ID"
  );

failures.question: Which active district has the highest average score in Reading?
failures.gold_sql: SELECT T1.District FROM schools AS T1 INNER JOIN satscores AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.cds WHERE T1.StatusType = 'Active' ORDER BY T2.AvgScrRead DESC LIMIT 1
failures.final_sql:
SELECT DISTINCT dname
FROM satscores
JOIN schools ON satscores.cds = schools.CDSCode
WHERE schools.StatusType = 'Active'
ORDER BY satscores.AvgScrRead DESC
LIMIT 1;

history.sql:

failures.question: Among all the lap records set on various circuits, what is the time for the fastest one?
failures.gold_sql: SELECT time FROM lapTimes ORDER BY (CASE WHEN INSTR(time, ':') <> INSTR(SUBSTR(time, INSTR(time, ':') + 1), ':') + INSTR(time, ':') THEN CAST(SUBSTR(time, 1, INSTR(time, ':') - 1) AS REAL) * 3600 ELSE 0 END) + (CAST(SUBSTR(time, INSTR(time, ':') - 2 * (INSTR(time, ':') = INSTR(SUBSTR(time, INSTR(time, ':') + 1), ':') + INSTR(time, ':')), INSTR(time, ':') - 1) AS REAL) * 60) + (CAST(SUBSTR(time, INSTR(time, ':') + 1, INSTR(time, '.') - INSTR(time, ':') - 1) AS REAL)) + (CAST(SUBSTR(time, INSTR(time, '.') + 1) AS REAL) / 1000) ASC LIMIT 1

failures.question: In superheroes with missing weight data, calculate the difference between the number of superheroes with blue eyes and no eye color.
failures.gold_sql: SELECT SUM(CASE WHEN T2.id = 7 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) - SUM(CASE WHEN T2.id = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) FROM superhero AS T1 INNER JOIN colour AS T2 ON T1.eye_colour_id = T2.id WHERE T1.weight_kg = 0 OR T1.weight_kg is NULL

failures.question: Among the molecules with element Calcium, are they mostly carcinogenic or non carcinogenic?
failures.gold_sql: SELECT T2.label FROM atom AS T1 INNER JOIN molecule AS T2 ON T1.molecule_id = T2.molecule_id WHERE T1.element = 'ca' GROUP BY T2.label ORDER BY COUNT(T2.label) DESC LIMIT 1
failures.final_sql:
SELECT 
  CASE 
    WHEN SUM(CASE WHEN m.label = '+' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) > SUM(CASE WHEN m.label = '-' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) 
    THEN 'carcinogenic' 
    ELSE 'non carcinogenic' 
  END AS majority_label
FROM molecule m
JOIN atom a ON m.molecule_id = a.molecule_id
WHERE a.element = 'ca'
LIMIT 1;

history.sql:
SELECT 
  CASE 
    WHEN SUM(CASE WHEN m.label = '+' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) > SUM(CASE WHEN m.label = '-' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) 
    THEN 'carcinogenic' 
    ELSE 'non carcinogenic' 
  END AS majority_label
FROM molecule m
JOIN atom a ON m.molecule_id = a.molecule_id
WHERE a.element = 'ca';

history.issue: Question asks for ranking but SQL has no ORDER BY.

history.sql:
SELECT 
  CASE 
    WHEN SUM(CASE WHEN m.label = '+' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) > SUM(CASE WHEN m.label = '-' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) 
    THEN 'carcinogenic' 
    ELSE 'non carcinogenic' 
  END AS majority_label
FROM molecule m
JOIN atom a ON m.molecule_id = a.molecule_id
WHERE a.element = 'ca'
ORDER BY 
  SUM(CASE WHEN m.label = '+' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) DESC,
  SUM(CASE WHEN m.label = '-' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) DESC;

failures.question: Among posts by Harvey Motulsky and Noah Snyder, which one has higher popularity?
failures.gold_sql: SELECT T1.DisplayName FROM users AS T1 INNER JOIN postHistory AS T2 ON T1.Id = T2.UserId INNER JOIN posts AS T3 ON T2.PostId = T3.Id WHERE T1.DisplayName = 'Harvey Motulsky' OR T1.DisplayName = 'Noah Snyder' GROUP BY T1.DisplayName ORDER BY SUM(T3.ViewCount) DESC LIMIT 1
failures.final_sql:
SELECT p.Title
FROM posts p
JOIN users u ON p.OwnerUserId = u.Id
WHERE u.DisplayName IN ('Harvey Motulsky', 'Noah Snyder')
ORDER BY p.ViewCount DESC
LIMIT 1;

history.sql:
SELECT p.Title
FROM posts p
JOIN users u ON p.OwnerUserId = u.Id
WHERE u.DisplayName IN ('Harvey Motulsky', 'Noah Snyder')
ORDER BY p.ViewCount DESC
LIMIT 1;

failures.question: Mention the reputation of users who had obtained the badge on 7/19/2010 7:39:08 PM.
failures.gold_sql: SELECT T1.Reputation FROM users AS T1 INNER JOIN badges AS T2 ON T1.Id = T2.UserId WHERE T2.Date = '2010-07-19 19:39:08.0'
failures.final_sql:
SELECT DISTINCT u.Reputation
FROM users u
JOIN badges b ON u.Id = b.UserId
WHERE b.Date LIKE '2010-07-19 19:39:08%'

history.sql:
SELECT u.Reputation
FROM users u
JOIN badges b ON u.Id = b.UserId
WHERE b.Date LIKE '2010-07-19 19:39:08%'

history.issue: SQL returns duplicate rows from a JOIN; add DISTINCT for this list/attribute question.

history.sql:
SELECT DISTINCT u.Reputation
FROM users u
JOIN badges b ON u.Id = b.UserId
WHERE b.Date LIKE '2010-07-19 19:39:08%'
